semanticCore: Dying down, extinguishing, subsiding of fire, cooling, or loss of heat/flame

keyDerivations.meaning: To abate, subside, grow dim, cool down, or become extinguished (fire)

figurativeOrRareUsages: مَأْوَاهُمْ جَهَنَّمُ ۖ كُلَّمَا خَبَتْ زِدْنَاهُمْ سَعِيرًا (17:97): Their refuge is Hell; whenever it abates/subsides, We increase for them the blaze.
discardedNoise: Khiba' (tent) - related spelling but distinct root concept.
summaryEn: The root refers to a fire losing its flame or dying down (Khubuw), describing the relentless nature of Hell whose flames are immediately rekindled whenever they show signs of subsiding.
summaryAr: يشير هذا الجذر إلى انطفاء لهيب النار وهدوء جمرها وسكون وهجها (خبو)، واصفاً عقاب جهنم بأنه مستمر لا يخبو ولا يسكن إلا ليزاد اشتعالاً.
rawText: اخبى النَّارَ 4 He extinguished the fire; (S, K;) he allayed, or extinguished, the flaming, or blazing, of the fire. (JK, Msb.) === And [hence,] اخبى الحَرْبَ ‡ He extinguished [or allayed] the fire of the war. (K, TA.) And اخبى حِدَّةَ النَّاقَهِ ‡ He extinguished [or allayed] the sharpness, or irascibility, of the she-camel. (K, * TA.) خَبَتِ النَّارُ 1 , aor. تَخْبُو, inf. n. خُبُوٌّ (JK, S, Msb, K) and خَبْوٌ, (K,) The fire became allayed; subsided; (K;) became extinguished; (S, K;) the flaming, or blazing, of the fire ceased, or became allayed or extinguished. (JK, Msb.) [The verb is used, in this sense, of Hell, in the Kur xvii. 99.] === [Hence,] خَبَتِ الحَرْبُ ‡ The war became allayed, assuaged, or appeased; (JK, K, TA;) became extinguished. (K, TA.) And خَبَتْ حِدَّةُ النَّاقَةِ ‡ The sharpness, or irascibility, of the she-camel became allayed, assuaged, or appeased. (JK, K, * TA.) And خَبَا لَهَبُهُ ‡ The heat, or vehemence, of his anger became allayed, assuaged, or appeased. (TA.) خِبَآءٌ accord. to some belongs to this art. (TA in art. خبأ.) See arts. خبأ and خبى.
